message:
feat(encode): add fallible try_* async encode API without breaking the panicking variants

Adds Result-returning counterparts to the three async encode entry
points that previously only existed in panic-on-error form. Keeps the
old signatures callable and documented, so existing call sites compile
unchanged; new callers that need to surface errors can switch to the
`try_*` path.

New fallible API:
- AsyncEncodeStream::try_new(input, options) -> Result<Self>
- try_encode_lines_async(input, options) -> Result<Vec<String>>
- try_encode_async(input, options) -> Result<String>
- try_encode_events_async(input, options) -> Result<Vec<JsonStreamEvent>>

Existing panicking functions now delegate to their `try_*` siblings
and `.expect("… failed")` on error, with `# Panics` sections added to
their rustdoc so the failure mode is documented rather than implicit.
Behavior when the pipeline succeeds is byte-identical to before.

Supporting changes:
- Add `use crate::error::Result` at the top of the module.
- `AsyncEncodeStream::{len, is_empty}` marked `const` (clippy nudge
  the fallible path made more obvious to rework).
- try_encode_events_async is annotated `#[allow(clippy::unused_async)]`
  because the function is `async` only for API parity with its
  streaming siblings; its body doesn't actually await anything.
- Existing encode_lines_async internal path now returns the `lines`
  as `Ok(lines)` so the streaming pipeline threads `?` through.

Rationale: the library's sync entry points have long offered both
`encode`/`try_encode` pairings; the async side should match so users
don't have to wrap every async call in `catch_unwind` to stay
resilient to malformed inputs.
